steps.title: Identify the contrast Harper criticizes
steps.content: The claim describes activists championing humanitarian causes in other countries while overlooking local concerns. The correct quotation must directly present this contrast—attention to distant causes paired with inattention to home-front suffering.

steps.title: Match the contrast to the choices
steps.content:
Choice B presents the exact opposition: “When ye plead for the wrecked and fallen, / The exile from far-distant shores, / Remember that men are still wasting / Life's crimson around your own doors.” The phrase “far-distant shores” names the foreign focus, and “your own doors” names the local suffering being overlooked. The speaker's demand to “remember” the home-front tragedy embodies Harper's criticism directly.

Choices A, C, and D lack this foreign-versus-domestic contrast—A focuses on divine justice, C offers patriotic blessing, and D describes song's power.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
